basicAuthUser.description: The username of the data source account

authType.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

autoCancel.description: Jobs automatically cancel after this many seconds of inactivity. Set to 0 to never auto cancel

dataLinks.description: Add links to existing fields. Links will be shown in log row details next to the field value.

datasourceUid.description: UID of a Grafana data source. When set, the derived data link becomes an internal link to that data source and the URL is interpreted as a query.

field.description: Can be exact field name or a regex pattern that will match on the field name.

label.description: Use to provide a meaningful label to the data matched in the regex

matcherRegex.description: Use to parse and capture some part of the log message. You can use the captured groups in the template.

defaultEarliestTime.description: Earliest time for searches without time range (such as template variable queries)

fieldSearchType.description: Set the Search Mode for Fields

internalFieldPattern.description: Regular expression defining the filter for filtering out internal fields

internalFieldsFiltration.description: By default hides fields with names that start with ‘_’. This filter can be customized using the Internal field pattern option.

keepCookies.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

maxPollInterval.description: Sets the bounds for async query poll interval. Each poll will run a random number milliseconds from [min, max).

maxResultCount.description: Set the `count` limit for returned results from each data request. When set to 0, there is no limit.

minPollInterval.description: Sets the bounds for async query poll interval. Each poll will run a random number milliseconds from [min, max).

oauthPassThru.description: Set to true only when the 'Forward OAuth Identity' (OAuthForward) authentication method is selected. Written by the config editor alongside jsonData.authType (SplunkAuthComponent.tsx:14-23); consumed by Grafana core to forward the user's OAuth token, not by the plugin backend. The OAuthForward method is only offered in the editor when the 'splunkEnableOAuthForwarding' feature toggle is enabled.

pollSearchResult.description: Run query and periodically check for results.

previewMode.description: Displays events as soon as the event is returned, instead of waiting until all of the events are returned.

serverName.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

statusBuckets.description: Maximum number of timeline status buckets generated per query. Set to 0 to disable timelines

streamMode.description: Legacy 'stream mode' flag no longer rendered by the config editor. The backend migrates a true value into previewMode (pkg/models/settings.go:103-105) and the frontend query builder also treats it as preview mode (src/datasource/query.ts:267). Preserved for round-trip fidelity with older datasources.

timeoutInSeconds.description: Grafana request timeout. This is the maximum time in seconds that a query can run before it is killed. Minimum value is 1.

tlsAuth.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

tlsAuthWithCACert.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

tlsSkipVerify.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

tsField.description: Field used by Grafana to determine event timestamps

variableSearchLevel.description: Set the Search Mode for Variables

url.description: Specify a complete HTTP URL (for example https://example.com:8080)

secureValues.description: The password of the data source account
Instead of providing a username and password, use an authentication token generated in Splunk dashboard.
The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed
The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: Basic authentication against the Splunk management/REST API. root.url, root.basicAuthUser and secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword (empty here) must be filled in to get a working datasource.

alternativeToken.description: Authenticate with a Splunk authentication token instead of a username/password. The token is stored in secureJsonData.authToken and sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' (pkg/splunk/client.go:229-230).

basicAuth.description: Authenticate with a Splunk username and password. The username is stored at root.basicAuthUser and the password in secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; the backend derives BasicAuthEnabled from authType (pkg/models/settings.go:95), so root.basicAuth is not set.

basicAuthWithTLS.summary: Basic authentication with TLS client auth and custom CA
basicAuthWithTLS.description: Basic auth combined with mutual TLS (jsonData.tlsAuth + serverName + client cert/key) and custom-CA verification (jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert + CA cert). TLS values are consumed by the SDK via config.HTTPClientOptions (pkg/models/settings.go:125).

secureJsonData.tlsClientKey:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
<redacted>
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

oauthForward.description: Forward the signed-in Grafana user's OAuth identity to Splunk. Sets jsonData.authType='OAuthForward' and jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; there is no secret to configure. Only offered in the editor when the splunkEnableOAuthForwarding feature toggle is enabled.

tokenWithAdvancedOptions.summary: Alternative token auth with advanced options and data links
tokenWithAdvancedOptions.description: A fully-populated instance: token auth, preview + async queries with a poll interval, a plugin request timeout, internal-field filtering, a custom result limit, forwarded cookies, and two data links (one external URL template, one internal link to a Tempo datasource).
